---
title: "Influenza in Madagascar and Africa"
subtitle: "GISAID EpiFlu Metadata Analysis"
author: "Camila Duitama"
date: today
format:
  html:
    embed-resources: true
    output-file: "index.html"
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    toc-title: "Contents"
    number-sections: true
    theme: cosmo
    fig-width: 10
    fig-height: 6
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

```{python}
#| label: setup

from pathlib import Path
import pandas as pd
import plotly.express as px
import plotly.graph_objects as go

DATA_DIR = Path("../data")

df = pd.read_csv(DATA_DIR / "combined_metadata.tsv", sep="\t", parse_dates=["Collection_Date"])

africa = df[df["Continent"] == "Africa"].copy()
mdg    = df[df["Madagascar"] == True].copy()
rest   = df[(df["Continent"] == "Africa") & (df["Madagascar"] == False)].copy()

for d in [df, africa, mdg, rest]:
    d["Subtype"] = d["Subtype"].fillna("Unknown")

PALETTE    = px.colors.qualitative.Bold
MDG_COLOR  = PALETTE[1]
REST_COLOR = PALETTE[5]
```

## Overview

This report summarises all influenza sequences available in
[GISAID EpiFlu](https://gisaid.org/epiflu-applications/epiflu-database/)
for **Africa (all hosts, all types)** with a focus on **Madagascar**.
Data were downloaded on **16 March 2026**.

A sample is considered **analysis-ready** if it meets all three criteria:

1. **Complete genome** — all 8 segments (PB2, PB1, PA, HA, NP, NA, MP, NS) present in the downloaded FASTA and each segment above minimum length thresholds (PB2/PB1 ≥ 1,800 nt, PA ≥ 1,700, HA ≥ 1,300, NP ≥ 1,200, NA ≥ 1,000, MP ≥ 800, NS ≥ 700)
2. **Known geographic location** — Country field non-empty
3. **Known sampling date** — Collection date present and plausible (1990–2026). All isolates in this dataset have full year-month-day precision (0 are year-only or month-only).

The two GISAID downloads are merged and deduplicated on `Isolate_Id`, with the most specific download taking precedence.

```{python}
#| label: tbl-overview

def completeness_row(label, d):
    n    = len(d)
    pr   = int(d["phylo_ready"].sum()) if "phylo_ready" in d.columns else 0
    loc  = int(d["Country"].notna().sum())
    yr   = int(d["Year"].between(1990, 2026).sum())
    ar   = int((d["phylo_ready"] & d["Country"].notna() & d["Year"].between(1990, 2026)).sum()) if "phylo_ready" in d.columns else 0
    dr_min = d["Collection_Date"].min()
    dr_max = d["Collection_Date"].max()
    date_range = (
        f"{dr_min.date()} → {dr_max.date()}"
        if pd.notna(dr_min) and pd.notna(dr_max) else "—"
    )
    return {
        "Dataset":                    label,
        "Isolates":                   f"{n:,}",
        "Complete genome (phylo-ready)": f"{pr:,} ({pr/n*100:.0f}%)",
        "Known location":             f"{loc:,} ({loc/n*100:.0f}%)",
        "Known date":                 f"{yr:,} ({yr/n*100:.0f}%)",
        "Analysis-ready (all 3 criteria)": f"{ar:,} ({ar/n*100:.0f}%)",
        "Date range":                 date_range,
    }

overview = pd.DataFrame([
    completeness_row("Madagascar", mdg),
    completeness_row("Rest of Africa", rest),
    completeness_row("All Africa", africa),
])

overview.to_html(index=False, classes="table table-sm table-bordered", border=0)
```

---

## Africa — All hosts

### Isolates per country

```{python}
#| label: fig-africa-map

country_counts = (
    africa.groupby("Country")
    .agg(
        Isolates=("Isolate_Id", "count"),
        Phylo=("phylo_ready", "sum"),
    )
    .reset_index()
)
country_counts["Phylo_pct"] = (
    country_counts["Phylo"] / country_counts["Isolates"] * 100
).round(1)

fig = px.choropleth(
    country_counts,
    locations="Country",
    locationmode="country names",
    color="Isolates",
    hover_name="Country",
    hover_data={"Isolates": True, "Phylo_pct": True, "Country": False},
    color_continuous_scale="YlOrRd",
    scope="africa",
    title="Influenza isolates per African country — all hosts (GISAID EpiFlu)",
    labels={"Phylo_pct": "Analysis-ready (%)"},
)
fig.update_geos(bgcolor="lightgrey", showcountries=True, countrycolor="white")
fig.show()
```

### Isolates per country — bar chart (top 15)

```{python}
#| label: fig-africa-bar

top15 = country_counts.nlargest(15, "Isolates").sort_values("Isolates", ascending=True)
top15["Color"] = top15["Country"].apply(lambda c: MDG_COLOR if c == "Madagascar" else REST_COLOR)

fig = go.Figure(go.Bar(
    x=top15["Isolates"],
    y=top15["Country"],
    orientation="h",
    marker_color=list(top15["Color"]),
    text=top15["Isolates"],
    textposition="outside",
))
fig.update_layout(
    title="Top 15 African countries by influenza isolate count — all hosts",
    xaxis_title="Isolates",
    yaxis_title="",
    showlegend=False,
    height=480,
)
fig.show()
```

### Sequences over time (Africa)

```{python}
#| label: fig-africa-timeline

af_t = africa[africa["Year"].between(1995, 2026)].copy()
top_sub = af_t["Subtype"].value_counts().head(7).index.tolist()
af_t["Subtype_grp"] = af_t["Subtype"].apply(lambda s: s if s in top_sub else "Other")

counts = af_t.groupby(["Year", "Subtype_grp"]).size().reset_index(name="n")

fig = px.bar(
    counts,
    x="Year", y="n",
    color="Subtype_grp",
    barmode="stack",
    color_discrete_sequence=PALETTE,
    labels={"n": "Isolates", "Subtype_grp": "Subtype"},
    title="Africa — influenza isolates per year by subtype (all hosts)",
)
fig.update_layout(legend_title_text="Subtype")
fig.show()
```

### Host distribution (Africa)

```{python}
#| label: fig-africa-host

host_counts = africa["Host"].value_counts().reset_index()
host_counts.columns = ["Host", "n"]

fig = px.bar(
    host_counts,
    x="Host", y="n",
    color="Host",
    color_discrete_sequence=PALETTE,
    text_auto=True,
    title="Africa — isolates by host (all hosts)",
    labels={"n": "Isolates"},
)
fig.update_layout(showlegend=False)
fig.show()
```

### Subtype distribution (Africa)

```{python}
#| label: fig-africa-subtype

top_sub_a = africa["Subtype"].value_counts().head(10).index.tolist()
africa["Subtype_grp"] = africa["Subtype"].apply(lambda s: s if s in top_sub_a else "Other")
sub_counts = africa["Subtype_grp"].value_counts().reset_index()
sub_counts.columns = ["Subtype", "n"]

fig = px.bar(
    sub_counts.sort_values("n", ascending=False),
    x="Subtype", y="n",
    color="Subtype",
    color_discrete_sequence=PALETTE,
    text_auto=True,
    title="Africa — isolates by subtype (all hosts)",
    labels={"n": "Isolates"},
)
fig.update_layout(showlegend=False)
fig.show()
```

### Influenza B lineage (Africa)

Among the **`{python} int((africa['Subtype']=='B').sum())`** Influenza B isolates from Africa,
the Victoria lineage predominates. A small fraction have no lineage annotation in GISAID.

```{python}
#| label: fig-africa-b-lineage

b_africa = africa[africa["Subtype"] == "B"].copy()
b_africa["Lineage_grp"] = b_africa["Lineage"].fillna("Unknown")
lin_a = b_africa["Lineage_grp"].value_counts().reset_index()
lin_a.columns = ["Lineage", "n"]

fig = px.pie(
    lin_a,
    names="Lineage", values="n",
    color_discrete_sequence=PALETTE,
    title="Africa — Influenza B lineage",
    hole=0.4,
)
fig.update_traces(textinfo="label+percent+value")
fig.show()
```

### Analysis-ready isolates by country (top 10)

```{python}
#| label: fig-africa-comp-country

top10 = africa["Country"].value_counts().head(10).index.tolist()
af_t10 = africa[africa["Country"].isin(top10)].copy()

comp_c = (
    af_t10.groupby(["Country", "phylo_ready"])
    .size().reset_index(name="n")
)
comp_c["Label"] = comp_c["phylo_ready"].map({True: "Analysis-ready", False: "Incomplete"})

fig = px.bar(
    comp_c,
    x="Country", y="n",
    color="Label",
    color_discrete_map={"Analysis-ready": PALETTE[2], "Incomplete": PALETTE[5]},
    barmode="stack",
    category_orders={"Country": top10},
    labels={"n": "Isolates", "Label": "Status"},
    title="Africa — analysis-ready isolates by country (top 10)",
)
fig.show()
```

---

## Madagascar

### Host distribution

```{python}
#| label: fig-mdg-host

host_mdg = mdg["Host"].value_counts().reset_index()
host_mdg.columns = ["Host", "n"]

fig = px.bar(
    host_mdg,
    x="Host", y="n",
    color="Host",
    color_discrete_sequence=PALETTE,
    text_auto=True,
    title="Madagascar — isolates by host",
    labels={"n": "Isolates"},
)
fig.update_layout(showlegend=False)
fig.show()
```

### Sequences over time by subtype

```{python}
#| label: fig-mdg-timeline

mdg_t = mdg[mdg["Year"].between(1990, 2026)].copy()
top_st = mdg_t["Subtype"].value_counts().head(6).index.tolist()
mdg_t["Subtype_grp"] = mdg_t["Subtype"].apply(lambda s: s if s in top_st else "Other")

counts_m = mdg_t.groupby(["Year", "Subtype_grp"]).size().reset_index(name="n")

fig = px.bar(
    counts_m,
    x="Year", y="n",
    color="Subtype_grp",
    barmode="stack",
    color_discrete_sequence=PALETTE,
    labels={"n": "Isolates", "Subtype_grp": "Subtype"},
    title="Madagascar — influenza isolates per year by subtype",
)
fig.update_layout(legend_title_text="Subtype", xaxis_title="Collection year")
fig.show()
```

### Subtype distribution

```{python}
#| label: fig-mdg-subtype

top_sub_m = mdg["Subtype"].value_counts().head(10).index.tolist()
mdg["Subtype_grp"] = mdg["Subtype"].apply(lambda s: s if s in top_sub_m else "Other")
sub_m = mdg["Subtype_grp"].value_counts().reset_index()
sub_m.columns = ["Subtype", "n"]

fig = px.bar(
    sub_m.sort_values("n", ascending=False),
    x="Subtype", y="n",
    color="Subtype",
    color_discrete_sequence=PALETTE,
    text_auto=True,
    title="Madagascar — isolates by subtype",
    labels={"n": "Isolates"},
)
fig.update_layout(showlegend=False)
fig.show()
```

### Influenza B lineage (Madagascar)

All **`{python} int((mdg['Subtype']=='B').sum())`** Influenza B isolates from Madagascar
have a lineage annotation. The Victoria lineage accounts for the vast majority.

```{python}
#| label: fig-mdg-b-lineage

b_mdg = mdg[mdg["Subtype"] == "B"].copy()
b_mdg["Lineage_grp"] = b_mdg["Lineage"].fillna("Unknown")
lin_m = b_mdg["Lineage_grp"].value_counts().reset_index()
lin_m.columns = ["Lineage", "n"]

fig = px.pie(
    lin_m,
    names="Lineage", values="n",
    color_discrete_sequence=PALETTE,
    title="Madagascar — Influenza B lineage",
    hole=0.4,
)
fig.update_traces(textinfo="label+percent+value")
fig.show()
```

### Analysis-ready isolates

```{python}
#| label: fig-mdg-completeness

comp_m = mdg.groupby("phylo_ready").size().reset_index(name="n")
comp_m["Label"] = comp_m["phylo_ready"].map(
    {True: "Analysis-ready (complete genome + loc + date)", False: "Not analysis-ready"}
)

fig = px.pie(
    comp_m,
    names="Label", values="n",
    color_discrete_sequence=[PALETTE[2], PALETTE[5]],
    title="Madagascar — analysis-ready isolates",
    hole=0.4,
)
fig.show()
```

### Analysis-ready isolates by subtype (top 6)

```{python}
#| label: fig-mdg-comp-subtype

top6_m = mdg["Subtype"].value_counts().head(6).index.tolist()
mdg_top = mdg[mdg["Subtype"].isin(top6_m)].copy()

fig = px.histogram(
    mdg_top,
    x="Subtype",
    color="phylo_ready",
    barmode="stack",
    color_discrete_map={True: PALETTE[2], False: PALETTE[5]},
    labels={"phylo_ready": "Analysis-ready"},
    title="Madagascar — analysis-ready isolates by subtype (top 6)",
    category_orders={"Subtype": top6_m},
)
fig.update_layout(yaxis_title="Isolates", xaxis_title="Subtype")
fig.show()
```

### Norosoa — Unpublished Avian Sequences

Unpublished avian influenza sequences collected by **Norosoa Razanajatovo**
(Institut Pasteur de Madagascar, 2021–2023). These 109 isolates (830 segment sequences)
are not publicly available in GISAID (except 3 partially-released H9N2 isolates) and
represent the **only near-complete avian influenza genomic data from Madagascar** in this
analysis.

**Data quality notes:**

- **3 isolates** (78609-23, 78830-23, 79149-23 — H9N2, July 2023) exist in GISAID with only
  5 segments. The Norosoa FASTAs contain CDC-sequenced complete genomes and supersede those
  entries in the alignments.
- **20 isolates are partially typed** (N2 and N6 files): only the NA segment was sequenced;
  HA subtype is unknown. Included in NA and internal-segment trees only.
- **1 confirmed co-infection** (H1N2/H6N1): sequences retained under the dominant subtype.
- **1 imported case** (80824-23, Chicken, H4N6, 22 Aug 2023, region "Brésil"): day-old chick
  imported from Brazil — excluded from African diversity assessments.

```{python}
#| label: norosoa-setup
#| include: false

CANONICAL       = ["PB2", "PB1", "PA", "HA", "NP", "NA", "MP", "NS"]
NOROSOA_FILES   = sorted(DATA_DIR.glob("norosoa_avian_*.fasta"))

nor_records = []
for fpath in NOROSOA_FILES:
    with open(fpath) as fh:
        for line in fh:
            if not line.startswith(">"):
                continue
            parts = line[1:].strip().split("/")
            if len(parts) < 5:
                continue
            tail = parts[4].split("_")
            nor_records.append({
                "isolate_id": parts[3],
                "host":       parts[1],
                "year":       int(tail[0]) if tail[0].isdigit() else None,
                "subtype":    tail[1] if len(tail) > 1 and tail[1] else "Unknown",
                # segment field is absent in H6 and H9 files (trailing underscore only)
                "segment":    tail[2] if len(tail) > 2 and tail[2] else None,
            })

nor_seqs = pd.DataFrame(nor_records)

# Sequences-per-isolate: robust proxy for genome completeness even when
# the segment field is absent from the header (H6, H9 files).
seqs_per_iso = nor_seqs.groupby("isolate_id").size().rename("n_seqs")

# Per-isolate metadata (take first occurrence for year/subtype/host)
nor_iso = (
    nor_seqs.drop_duplicates("isolate_id")
    [["isolate_id", "year", "subtype", "host"]]
    .set_index("isolate_id")
    .join(seqs_per_iso)
    .reset_index()
)
nor_iso["complete"] = nor_iso["n_seqs"] >= 7  # ≥7/8 segments present
```

#### Subtype distribution

```{python}
#| label: fig-nor-subtype

sub_nor = nor_iso["subtype"].value_counts().reset_index()
sub_nor.columns = ["Subtype", "Isolates"]

fig = px.bar(
    sub_nor.sort_values("Isolates", ascending=False),
    x="Subtype", y="Isolates",
    color="Subtype",
    color_discrete_sequence=PALETTE,
    text_auto=True,
    title="Norosoa — isolate count by subtype (109 unique isolates)",
    labels={"Isolates": "Isolates (unique)"},
)
fig.update_layout(showlegend=False)
fig.show()
```

#### Sampling timeline

```{python}
#| label: fig-nor-timeline

nor_t = nor_iso.dropna(subset=["year"]).copy()
nor_t["year"] = nor_t["year"].astype(int)
counts_nor = nor_t.groupby(["year", "subtype"]).size().reset_index(name="n")

fig = px.bar(
    counts_nor,
    x="year", y="n",
    color="subtype",
    barmode="stack",
    color_discrete_sequence=PALETTE,
    labels={"n": "Isolates", "subtype": "Subtype", "year": "Collection year"},
    title="Norosoa — isolates per year by subtype",
    text_auto=True,
)
fig.update_layout(
    xaxis=dict(tickmode="array", tickvals=sorted(nor_t["year"].unique())),
)
fig.show()
```

#### Genome completeness

Each isolate may have been sequenced for some or all 8 gene segments
(PB2, PB1, PA, HA, NP, NA, MP, NS). Completeness is estimated from the
number of sequences per isolate in the FASTA files
(≥ 7 sequences ≈ complete genome).

```{python}
#| label: fig-nor-completeness

comp_nor = (
    nor_iso.groupby(["subtype", "complete"])
    .size().reset_index(name="n")
)
comp_nor["Status"] = comp_nor["complete"].map(
    {True: "≥7 segments (near-complete)", False: "Partial (< 7 segments)"}
)

fig = px.bar(
    comp_nor,
    x="subtype", y="n",
    color="Status",
    barmode="stack",
    color_discrete_map={
        "≥7 segments (near-complete)": PALETTE[2],
        "Partial (< 7 segments)":      PALETTE[5],
    },
    labels={"n": "Isolates", "subtype": "Subtype", "Status": "Completeness"},
    title="Norosoa — genome completeness by subtype",
    text_auto=True,
)
fig.update_layout(xaxis_title="Subtype", yaxis_title="Isolates")
fig.show()
```

---

## Sequence Quality

For each isolate with sequences, we computed the length of every segment
available in the FASTA files. This section shows whether segments are long enough for phylogenetic analysis.

### Segment length distribution (Africa, complete genomes)

```{python}
#| label: fig-seg-lengths-africa

SEGMENTS = ["PB2", "PB1", "PA", "HA", "NP", "NA", "MP", "NS"]
MIN_LEN = {"PB2": 1800, "PB1": 1800, "PA": 1700,
            "HA": 1300, "NP": 1200, "NA": 1000, "MP": 800, "NS": 700}

# Only isolates with at least one sequence
af_seq = africa[africa["n_segs_sequenced"] > 0].copy()

# Melt to long format
length_cols = [f"{s}_length" for s in SEGMENTS if f"{s}_length" in af_seq.columns]
long = af_seq[["Isolate_Id", "Country"] + length_cols].melt(
    id_vars=["Isolate_Id", "Country"],
    var_name="Segment", value_name="Length"
).dropna(subset=["Length"])
long["Segment"] = long["Segment"].str.replace("_length", "")

# Add minimum-length threshold line
thresholds = pd.DataFrame([
    {"Segment": s, "Min_length": v} for s, v in MIN_LEN.items()
])

fig = px.box(
    long,
    x="Segment", y="Length",
    color="Segment",
    color_discrete_sequence=PALETTE,
    category_orders={"Segment": SEGMENTS},
    title="Africa — segment length distribution (all isolates with sequences)",
    labels={"Length": "Sequence length (nt)"},
    points=False,
)
# Add min-length threshold markers
for _, row in thresholds.iterrows():
    fig.add_shape(type="line",
        xref="x", yref="y",
        x0=row["Segment"], x1=row["Segment"],
        y0=row["Min_length"] - 30, y1=row["Min_length"] + 30,
        line=dict(color="red", width=3))
fig.add_scatter(x=[None], y=[None], mode="markers",
    marker=dict(color="red", size=8, symbol="line-ew"),
    name="Minimum length threshold (analysis-ready cutoff)")
fig.show()
```

### Phylo-ready isolates per country (top 10)

```{python}
#| label: fig-phylo-country

top10_p = africa["Country"].value_counts().head(10).index.tolist()
af_p = africa[africa["Country"].isin(top10_p)].copy()

comp_p = (
    af_p.groupby(["Country", "phylo_ready"])
    .size().reset_index(name="n")
)
comp_p["Label"] = comp_p["phylo_ready"].map({True: "Phylo-ready", False: "Not phylo-ready"})

fig = px.bar(
    comp_p,
    x="Country", y="n",
    color="Label",
    color_discrete_map={"Phylo-ready": PALETTE[2], "Not phylo-ready": PALETTE[5]},
    barmode="stack",
    category_orders={"Country": top10_p},
    labels={"n": "Isolates", "Label": "Status"},
    title="Africa — phylo-ready isolates by country (top 10)",
)
fig.show()
```

---

## Phylogenetic Analysis

16 maximum-likelihood trees (IQ-TREE 3.1.0, HKY+G) were built across all segment × subtype
combinations with ≥ 10 Madagascar sequences. Monophyletic Madagascar clades were detected
with `scripts/analyse_trees.py` (ete3, midpoint-rooted, maximal all-Madagascar subtrees ≥ 2 tips).

**Key finding:** 74–100% of Madagascar sequences fall into local monophyletic clades
depending on subtype. Avian H6N2 and H9N2 show founder-effect patterns (1–2 large clades,
high stem branch lengths ~0.02–0.03), while seasonal human subtypes show many small clades
consistent with annual reintroduction.

→ **[Full phylogenetic analysis, tree visualizations and clade statistics](trees.html)**

---

## Data Sources

- **GISAID EpiFlu** (<https://gisaid.org>) — data downloaded on **16 March 2026**.
  Madagascar dataset: all hosts, all influenza types.
  Africa dataset: all hosts, all African countries, all influenza types.

- **Norosoa Razanajatovo** (Institut Pasteur de Madagascar) — unpublished avian influenza
  sequences from Madagascar, collected 2021–2023, provided directly to this analysis.
  109 unique isolates, 830 segment sequences, covering subtypes H1N2, H4, H6N2, H6N8,
  H7N7, H9N2 and partial-subtype isolates (N2, N6). Complete genomes assembled from
  CDC sequencing. Not yet publicly available in GISAID (3 partial H9N2 entries exist:
  78609-23, 78830-23, 79149-23 — superseded by these complete genomes in this analysis).
